judgments.comparative_verdict: Both reports are comparably thorough and hit all four expectations with concrete file/line citations; Report 2 is marginally more decisive on removing the legacy secret and on the migration remediation ask, but the difference is minor.

report_1.evidence: ENABLE_DEBUG_TOOLBAR = "true" ... Change to "false". AUTH_RELAXED = "true" ... Change to "false". PRESET_ADMIN_ACCOUNTS = "qa@example.com:superadmin" — pre-seeds a QA account as superadmin in production. Change to "" (empty...)

report_2.evidence: ENABLE_DEBUG_TOOLBAR = "true" (infra/prod/app.auto.tfvars:2) ... Remove the line entirely ... AUTH_RELAXED = "true" ... Remove entirely ... PRESET_ADMIN_ACCOUNTS = "qa@example.com:superadmin" ... Remove entirely

report_1.evidence: that URL is baked into the JavaScript bundle at build time — it is not a runtime setting. ... The deploy-prod job (lines 29-35) deploys that same digest to prod. Consequence: the production frontend, as configured, would ship with the staging API base URL hard-coded into it ... fixing this requires both adding that file with the production API URL and changing the pipeline — either a separate production build/image or a runtime-injected config

report_2.evidence: VITE_-prefixed variables are inlined into the bundle at build time ... the resulting digest is deployed first to staging (line 28) and then — the same, already-built digest — to prod (lines 34-35). Consequence: the production frontend would ship with https://staging-api.example.com baked into its JavaScript, with no runtime fix possible. ... Prod needs its own build (--mode production with a new frontend/.env.production pointing at the prod API)

report_1.evidence: No migration step is visible anywhere in the pipeline, yet docs/prod-runbook.md ... claims "Database migrations are applied automatically during deploy." With three migrations pending for a brand-new prod database ... and no deploy.sh to inspect, there is no verifiable mechanism that would apply them on first launch.

report_2.evidence: No migration step: the workflow contains no reference to migrations (grep confirms), despite docs/prod-runbook.md:6 claiming "Database migrations are applied automatically during deploy." The runbook ... its claim is contradicted by the live workflow — on a fresh prod database, migrations 0001_base–0003_billing would need a first manual pass or an added pipeline step.

report_1.evidence: the not-yet-applied infra/prod/main.tf:3 still trusts …environment:staging. So as committed, production CI cannot authenticate (its token won't match the trust condition), while staging jobs would be trusted by the production deploy role — the inverse of what's wanted. ... Change to repo:acme-health/medislot:environment:production so only jobs running in the production GitHub environment (with its approval gates) can assume the prod deploy role.

report_2.evidence: infra/prod/main.tf:3 was copied unedited and still provisions the prod deploy role to trust …:environment:staging. So as written, the production job can never assume the prod role ... Prod needs its own role name and its own environment:production trust condition, not one shared or OR'd with staging.
